history-starter.event-year.cudgel-war.prompt: In what year was the United States Declaration of Independence adopted?
history-starter.event-year.cudgel-war.explanation: The Continental Congress adopted the Declaration of Independence in 1776.
history-starter.event-year.cudgel-war.details: The delegates approved the Declaration on July 4, 1776, formally announcing that the thirteen colonies regarded themselves as independent states.

history-starter.treaty-year.japan-china-.prompt: What wedge-shaped writing system was used in ancient Mesopotamia?
history-starter.treaty-year.japan-china-.explanation: Cuneiform was the wedge-shaped writing system developed in ancient Mesopotamia.
history-starter.treaty-year.japan-china-.details: Scribes pressed reed styli into clay tablets to form wedge-shaped marks. Sumerians developed the system, and several later Mesopotamian languages adopted it.

history-starter.event-year.guangzhou-upr.prompt: In what year did Christopher Columbus first reach the Americas?
history-starter.event-year.guangzhou-upr.explanation: Columbus first reached islands in the Caribbean in 1492.
history-starter.event-year.guangzhou-upr.details: His voyage crossed the Atlantic under the sponsorship of the Spanish monarchs. Indigenous peoples had inhabited the Americas for thousands of years before his arrival.

history-starter.treaty-year.bangkok-rule.prompt: Which river was central to the agriculture and settlement of ancient Egypt?
history-starter.treaty-year.bangkok-rule.explanation: The Nile supported ancient Egyptian agriculture and settlement.
history-starter.treaty-year.bangkok-rule.details: Its annual inundation deposited fertile soil along the river valley, while the river also served as Egypt's principal transportation corridor.

history-starter.event-year.odz-insurrect.prompt: Which ancient Greek city-state is most closely associated with the development of democracy?
history-starter.event-year.odz-insurrect.explanation: Athens developed the best-known form of direct democracy in the ancient Greek world.
history-starter.event-year.odz-insurrect.details: Eligible male citizens debated and voted directly in the assembly. The system excluded women, enslaved people, and resident foreigners.

history-starter.treaty-year.munich-agree.prompt: In what year did the Norman conquest of England begin with the Battle of Hastings?
history-starter.treaty-year.munich-agree.explanation: William the Conqueror defeated Harold II at Hastings in 1066.
history-starter.treaty-year.munich-agree.details: The victory led to Norman rule in England and major changes in landholding, government, architecture, and the English language.

history-starter.event-year.greater-polan.prompt: In what year did the Berlin Wall fall?
history-starter.event-year.greater-polan.explanation: The Berlin Wall opened on November 9, 1989.
history-starter.event-year.greater-polan.details: An East German announcement about travel rules prompted crowds to gather at the crossings. The opening became a symbol of the collapse of communist rule in Eastern Europe.

history-starter.treaty-year.thomson-urru.prompt: Who became the first Roman emperor?
history-starter.treaty-year.thomson-urru.explanation: Augustus became Rome's first emperor after the collapse of the Roman Republic.
history-starter.treaty-year.thomson-urru.details: Born Gaius Octavius, he defeated his rivals and received the title Augustus in 27 BCE while preserving many outward forms of republican government.

history-starter.event-year.majimaji-war.prompt: Which two powers fought the Punic Wars?
history-starter.event-year.majimaji-war.explanation: Rome and Carthage fought the three Punic Wars for dominance in the western Mediterranean.
history-starter.event-year.majimaji-war.details: The conflicts included Hannibal's invasion of Italy and ended with Rome destroying Carthage in 146 BCE.

history-starter.treaty-year.protocol-ii.prompt: What city served as the capital of the Byzantine Empire?
history-starter.treaty-year.protocol-ii.explanation: Constantinople was the capital of the Byzantine Empire.
history-starter.treaty-year.protocol-ii.details: Constantine I refounded Byzantium as Constantinople in 330. Its strategic position on the Bosporus helped it remain an imperial capital until the Ottoman conquest of 1453.

history-starter.event-year.malabar-rebel.prompt: What name is given to the warrior class of feudal Japan?
history-starter.event-year.malabar-rebel.explanation: Samurai were the hereditary warrior class of premodern Japan.
history-starter.event-year.malabar-rebel.details: They served regional lords and later became a governing elite under the shogunates. Ronin were samurai who lacked a master.

history-starter.treaty-year.donation-of-.prompt: Which West African empire succeeded Mali as the region's dominant power?
history-starter.treaty-year.donation-of-.explanation: The Songhai Empire became the leading power of the western Sudan after Mali declined.
history-starter.treaty-year.donation-of-.details: Centered on the Niger River, Songhai reached its greatest extent under Sunni Ali and Askia Muhammad before a Moroccan invasion in 1591.

history-starter.event-year.1905-tibetan-.prompt: Which ancient empire built the Royal Road linking Susa with western Anatolia?
history-starter.event-year.1905-tibetan-.explanation: The Achaemenid Persian Empire maintained the Royal Road for imperial communication.
history-starter.event-year.1905-tibetan-.details: The road connected major administrative centers across thousands of kilometers, allowing royal couriers and officials to travel through a relay system.

history-starter.treaty-year.geneva-conve.prompt: In what year did King John agree to Magna Carta?
history-starter.treaty-year.geneva-conve.explanation: King John agreed to Magna Carta in 1215.
history-starter.treaty-year.geneva-conve.details: The charter was sealed at Runnymede after a rebellion by English barons. Later versions became enduring symbols of limited government and due process.

history-starter.event-year.revolution-of.prompt: Which ancient artifact enabled scholars to decipher Egyptian hieroglyphs?
history-starter.event-year.revolution-of.explanation: The Rosetta Stone provided parallel texts that helped scholars decipher Egyptian hieroglyphs.
history-starter.event-year.revolution-of.details: The decree appears in hieroglyphic, Demotic, and ancient Greek scripts. Jean-Francois Champollion used the inscriptions in his breakthrough decipherment.

history-starter.treaty-year.sino-japanes.prompt: In which city was the Abbasid House of Wisdom located?
history-starter.treaty-year.sino-japanes.explanation: The House of Wisdom was associated with Abbasid Baghdad.
history-starter.treaty-year.sino-japanes.details: The institution became linked with the translation and study of works in mathematics, astronomy, medicine, and philosophy during the Abbasid intellectual flowering.

history-starter.event-year.bava-beccaris.prompt: Which Norman leader conquered England after defeating Harold II?
history-starter.event-year.bava-beccaris.explanation: William the Conqueror became king of England after defeating Harold II.
history-starter.event-year.bava-beccaris.details: William, duke of Normandy, won the Battle of Hastings and was crowned in Westminster Abbey, beginning Norman rule in England.

history-starter.treaty-year.treaty-on-th.prompt: What kind of vessels carried Polynesian navigators across the Pacific?
history-starter.treaty-year.treaty-on-th.explanation: Polynesian voyagers crossed great ocean distances in sophisticated outrigger and double-hulled canoes.
history-starter.treaty-year.treaty-on-th.details: Navigators read stars, swells, winds, clouds, and wildlife without modern instruments, settling islands across a vast area of the Pacific.

history-starter.event-year.autumn-harves.prompt: What term describes the exchange of plants, animals, diseases, and peoples between the Americas and Afro-Eurasia after 1492?
history-starter.event-year.autumn-harves.explanation: The Columbian Exchange transformed societies and environments on both sides of the Atlantic.
history-starter.event-year.autumn-harves.details: Foods such as maize and potatoes spread eastward, while horses, wheat, and devastating Old World diseases moved westward.

history-starter.treaty-year.inter-americ.prompt: Which theologian's Ninety-five Theses became a catalyst for the Protestant Reformation?
history-starter.treaty-year.inter-americ.explanation: Martin Luther's Ninety-five Theses challenged the sale of indulgences and helped ignite the Reformation.
history-starter.treaty-year.inter-americ.details: The dispute expanded into a broader challenge to church authority and contributed to the creation of new Protestant traditions.

history-starter.event-year.tambov-rebell.prompt: Which Mughal emperor commissioned the Taj Mahal?
history-starter.event-year.tambov-rebell.explanation: Shah Jahan commissioned the Taj Mahal as a mausoleum for Mumtaz Mahal.
history-starter.event-year.tambov-rebell.details: The white-marble complex at Agra was built by teams of artisans during the 17th century and became a masterpiece of Mughal architecture.

history-starter.treaty-year.declaration-.prompt: What term describes the forced Atlantic voyage endured by enslaved Africans?
history-starter.treaty-year.declaration-.explanation: The Middle Passage was the brutal transatlantic journey endured by enslaved Africans.
history-starter.treaty-year.declaration-.details: Captives were confined aboard slave ships under deadly conditions as part of the transatlantic slave trade.

history-starter.event-year.musha-inciden.prompt: Which country emerged from the only successful large-scale revolt by enslaved people in modern history?
history-starter.event-year.musha-inciden.explanation: The Haitian Revolution defeated colonial rule and created independent Haiti.
history-starter.event-year.musha-inciden.details: Enslaved people in the French colony of Saint-Domingue began the uprising in 1791. Independence in 1804 created the first Black republic.

history-starter.treaty-year.optional-pro.prompt: In which country did the Industrial Revolution begin?
history-starter.treaty-year.optional-pro.explanation: The Industrial Revolution began in Great Britain before spreading elsewhere.
history-starter.treaty-year.optional-pro.details: Britain combined coal, capital, expanding markets, agricultural change, and technological innovation to pioneer mechanized production.

history-starter.treaty-year.cross-strait.prompt: Which Enlightenment thinker is closely associated with the separation of governmental powers?
history-starter.treaty-year.cross-strait.explanation: Montesquieu argued that political liberty was protected by separating legislative, executive, and judicial powers.
history-starter.treaty-year.cross-strait.details: His Spirit of the Laws influenced constitutional thought in Europe and the Americas, including the design of the United States government.

history-starter.event-year.jacobite-risi.prompt: Which leader is known as 'the Liberator' for independence campaigns across northern South America?
aliases: Simon Jose Antonio de la Santisima Trinidad Bolivar
history-starter.event-year.jacobite-risi.explanation: Simon Bolivar led independence campaigns across much of northern South America.
history-starter.event-year.jacobite-risi.details: His forces helped free territories that became Venezuela, Colombia, Ecuador, Peru, Bolivia, and Panama from Spanish rule.

history-starter.treaty-year.split-agreem.prompt: The Opium Wars were fought primarily between China and which European power?
history-starter.treaty-year.split-agreem.explanation: China fought Great Britain in the First Opium War and Britain and France in the Second.
history-starter.treaty-year.split-agreem.details: The conflicts forced Qing China to accept unequal treaties, open treaty ports, and surrender Hong Kong to Britain after the first war.

history-starter.event-year.bai-lang-rebe.prompt: The Berlin Conference of 1884-85 set rules for European colonization on which continent?
history-starter.event-year.bai-lang-rebe.explanation: The Berlin Conference established European rules for claiming territory in Africa.
history-starter.event-year.bai-lang-rebe.details: No African representatives participated. The conference accelerated the Scramble for Africa and the imposition of colonial borders.

history-starter.treaty-year.african-nucl.prompt: Which Russian emperor issued the emancipation decree that freed the serfs?
history-starter.treaty-year.african-nucl.explanation: Alexander II issued the 1861 emancipation decree ending serfdom in Russia.
history-starter.treaty-year.african-nucl.details: The reform granted legal freedom to millions of serfs, though land payments and communal restrictions limited its immediate benefits.

history-starter.event-year.slovak-uprisi.prompt: Which self-governing country was the first to grant women the right to vote in national elections?
history-starter.event-year.slovak-uprisi.explanation: New Zealand granted women voting rights in parliamentary elections in 1893.
history-starter.event-year.slovak-uprisi.details: The achievement followed years of organizing and petitions led by suffragists including Kate Sheppard.

history-starter.treaty-year.treaty-of-pr.prompt: Gandhi's Salt March protested a British monopoly and tax on what commodity?
history-starter.treaty-year.treaty-of-pr.explanation: The Salt March challenged British control and taxation of salt in colonial India.
history-starter.treaty-year.treaty-of-pr.details: Gandhi walked to the Arabian Sea and made salt in defiance of the law, inspiring a broader campaign of civil disobedience.

history-starter.event-year.coluna-preste.prompt: Who founded the Republic of Turkey and served as its first president?
history-starter.event-year.coluna-preste.explanation: Mustafa Kemal Ataturk founded the Republic of Turkey and became its first president.
history-starter.event-year.coluna-preste.details: He led the Turkish national movement after World War I and introduced sweeping secular, legal, educational, and linguistic reforms.

history-starter.treaty-year.fourth-genev.prompt: Which Austrian statesman dominated the Congress of Vienna?
history-starter.treaty-year.fourth-genev.explanation: Austrian foreign minister Klemens von Metternich was the leading host and strategist at the Congress of Vienna.
history-starter.treaty-year.fourth-genev.details: The congress redrew Europe after the Napoleonic Wars and sought a balance of power that would contain both France and revolutionary movements.

history-starter.event-year.greater-polan.prompt: Who led the Taiping Rebellion and claimed to be the younger brother of Jesus?
history-starter.event-year.greater-polan.explanation: Hong Xiuquan founded and led the Taiping Heavenly Kingdom.
history-starter.event-year.greater-polan.details: His movement captured Nanjing and fought the Qing dynasty in one of history's deadliest civil wars before being defeated in 1864.

history-starter.treaty-year.treaty-of-ve.prompt: Which trade-union leader became the best-known figure of Poland's Solidarity movement?
history-starter.treaty-year.treaty-of-ve.explanation: Lech Walesa led the Solidarity trade-union movement in communist Poland.
history-starter.treaty-year.treaty-of-ve.details: Solidarity grew from strikes at the Gdansk shipyard into a mass social movement and helped drive Poland's negotiated transition from communist rule.

history-starter.event-year.march-action.prompt: Which Ethiopian victory over Italy became a major symbol of African resistance to colonialism?
history-starter.event-year.march-action.explanation: Ethiopia's victory at the Battle of Adwa preserved its independence from Italian conquest.
history-starter.event-year.march-action.details: Emperor Menelik II's forces defeated the invading Italian army in 1896, making Adwa a powerful symbol for later Pan-African movements.

history-starter.treaty-year.regional-roa.prompt: Which Vietnamese leader declared independence from France and led North Vietnam?
history-starter.treaty-year.regional-roa.explanation: Ho Chi Minh led the Vietnamese independence movement and became president of North Vietnam.
history-starter.treaty-year.regional-roa.details: He founded the Viet Minh, declared Vietnamese independence after World War II, and remained the central political symbol of North Vietnam until his death.

world-turning-points.turning-point-year..prompt: In what year did the French Revolution begin?
world-turning-points.turning-point-year..details: The Estates-General met in May, the National Assembly formed in June, and Parisians stormed the Bastille in July.

world-turning-points.treaty-year.quirina.prompt: Which empire ended when Ottoman forces captured Constantinople in 1453?
world-turning-points.treaty-year.quirina.explanation: The Ottoman capture of Constantinople ended the Byzantine Empire.
world-turning-points.treaty-year.quirina.details: Sultan Mehmed II's conquest brought the last remnant of the eastern Roman imperial state to an end and made the city an Ottoman capital.

world-turning-points.turning-point-year..prompt: In what year did the Bolsheviks seize power in Russia's October Revolution?
world-turning-points.turning-point-year..details: The October Revolution overthrew the provisional government and brought Lenin's Bolshevik Party to power, leading toward civil war and the creation of the Soviet Union.

world-turning-points.treaty-year.beijing.prompt: Who made nonviolent civil disobedience central to India's independence movement?
world-turning-points.treaty-year.beijing.explanation: Mahatma Gandhi made nonviolent mass resistance central to the Indian independence struggle.
world-turning-points.treaty-year.beijing.details: His campaigns included noncooperation, the Salt March, and Quit India, combining political action with the principle of satyagraha.

world-turning-points.turning-point-year..prompt: Which U.S. president launched the New Deal?
world-turning-points.turning-point-year..explanation: Franklin D. Roosevelt launched the New Deal in response to the Great Depression.
world-turning-points.turning-point-year..details: The programs expanded federal relief, regulation, public works, and social insurance while reshaping the relationship between the federal government and citizens.

world-turning-points.treaty-year.treaty-.prompt: Which financial collapse is commonly associated with the beginning of the Great Depression in the United States?
world-turning-points.treaty-year.treaty-.explanation: The Wall Street Crash of 1929 is commonly associated with the onset of the Great Depression.
world-turning-points.treaty-year.treaty-.details: The crash did not cause the worldwide depression by itself, but it damaged confidence and intensified banking, credit, production, and employment crises.

world-turning-points.turning-point-year..prompt: In what year did India gain independence from British rule?
world-turning-points.turning-point-year..details: British India was partitioned into the independent states of India and Pakistan, a process accompanied by mass migration and terrible communal violence.

world-turning-points.treaty-year.prum-co.prompt: The Marshall Plan provided American recovery aid primarily to which region?
world-turning-points.treaty-year.prum-co.explanation: The Marshall Plan funded postwar economic recovery in Western Europe.
world-turning-points.treaty-year.prum-co.details: The United States provided billions of dollars in assistance to rebuild production, trade, and infrastructure after World War II.

world-turning-points.turning-point-year..prompt: Which military alliance was created by the United States, Canada, and Western European countries in 1949?
world-turning-points.turning-point-year..explanation: NATO was created as a collective-defense alliance in 1949.
world-turning-points.turning-point-year..details: The North Atlantic Treaty states that an armed attack against one member will be considered an attack against them all.

world-turning-points.treaty-year.treaty-.prompt: Which international organization preceded the United Nations?
world-turning-points.treaty-year.treaty-.explanation: The League of Nations was the principal international organization created after World War I.
world-turning-points.treaty-year.treaty-.details: It sought to prevent war through collective security but lacked the participation and enforcement power needed to stop aggression in the 1930s.

world-turning-points.turning-point-year..prompt: Apartheid was a system of institutionalized racial segregation in which country?
world-turning-points.turning-point-year..explanation: Apartheid was imposed by South Africa's white-minority government.
world-turning-points.turning-point-year..details: The system classified people by race and controlled residence, movement, education, work, and political rights until democratic rule was established in 1994.

world-turning-points.treaty-year.treaty-.prompt: Whose arrest sparked the Montgomery bus boycott?
world-turning-points.treaty-year.treaty-.explanation: Rosa Parks's arrest became the immediate catalyst for the Montgomery bus boycott.
world-turning-points.treaty-year.treaty-.details: Black residents sustained the boycott for more than a year, and the campaign helped bring Martin Luther King Jr. to national prominence.

world-turning-points.turning-point-year..prompt: Which two superpowers confronted each other during the Cuban Missile Crisis?
world-turning-points.turning-point-year..explanation: The United States and Soviet Union confronted each other over Soviet missiles in Cuba.
world-turning-points.turning-point-year..details: The thirteen-day crisis brought the powers close to nuclear war before agreements led to the removal of the missiles.

world-turning-points.treaty-year.interna.prompt: Who led Ghana to independence and became its first prime minister?
world-turning-points.treaty-year.interna.explanation: Kwame Nkrumah led the Gold Coast to independence as Ghana.
world-turning-points.treaty-year.interna.details: Nkrumah became a major advocate of Pan-African unity and made Ghana the first sub-Saharan African colony to gain independence from European rule after World War II.

world-turning-points.turning-point-year..prompt: Algeria won independence after a long war against which colonial power?
world-turning-points.turning-point-year..explanation: Algeria gained independence after an eight-year war against France.
world-turning-points.turning-point-year..details: The National Liberation Front fought French forces in a bitter conflict that also produced political crisis in France and ended with the Evian Accords.

world-turning-points.treaty-year.slavery.prompt: The Nuremberg Trials prosecuted leading officials of which regime?
world-turning-points.treaty-year.slavery.explanation: The Nuremberg Trials prosecuted leading officials of Nazi Germany.
world-turning-points.treaty-year.slavery.details: The proceedings established influential precedents for individual responsibility for war crimes, crimes against humanity, and crimes against peace.

world-turning-points.turning-point-year..prompt: Which Egyptian leader nationalized the Suez Canal, helping trigger the Suez Crisis?
world-turning-points.turning-point-year..explanation: President Gamal Abdel Nasser nationalized the Suez Canal Company.
world-turning-points.turning-point-year..details: Britain, France, and Israel invaded Egypt, but international pressure forced their withdrawal and marked a major shift in postwar power.

world-turning-points.treaty-year.europea.prompt: Which reform-minded Czechoslovak leader became the symbol of the Prague Spring?
world-turning-points.treaty-year.europea.explanation: Alexander Dubcek led the Prague Spring reform movement.
world-turning-points.treaty-year.europea.details: His program of 'socialism with a human face' loosened censorship and sought political reform before Warsaw Pact forces invaded Czechoslovakia.

world-turning-points.turning-point-year..prompt: Which Yugoslav leader helped found the Non-Aligned Movement?
world-turning-points.turning-point-year..explanation: Josip Broz Tito was one of the principal founders of the Non-Aligned Movement.
world-turning-points.turning-point-year..details: Tito joined leaders including Jawaharlal Nehru, Gamal Abdel Nasser, Kwame Nkrumah, and Sukarno in seeking an independent path outside the Cold War blocs.

world-turning-points.treaty-year.eu-uk-t.prompt: Which dissident playwright became president after Czechoslovakia's Velvet Revolution?
world-turning-points.treaty-year.eu-uk-t.explanation: Vaclav Havel became president after the peaceful Velvet Revolution ended communist rule.
world-turning-points.treaty-year.eu-uk-t.details: Havel had been a playwright, essayist, and leading dissident associated with Charter 77 before becoming Czechoslovakia's last president and the Czech Republic's first.
